title: (國際線: 移居美國既巴絲請出手) - 一人一封遊說信比美國議員

replies.msg:
[ Address]
[Name of Legislators]

I am writing to sincerely request you to nominate Hong Kong freedom fighters for the 2020 Nobel Peace Prize.

Hong Kong has been under the spotlight in the past 70 days since there is an on-going social movement unprecedentedly affecting Hong Kong and China, and even the world. All things began with a proposed legislation, the Fugitive Offenders Ordinance. In February 2019, the Hong Kong Government proposed to establish a mechanism for extraditing any suspects, who are suspected of commiting any offence listed in the Fugitive Offenders Ordinance by the Chinese Government, to Mainland China. As we know the current Chinese judicial system is controlled by the Communist Party. Suspects do not enjoy fair and open trials. Individuals, such as the Party’s political opponents and activists, have forcibly “disappeared”, falsely accused, and lawyers have been arrested and even tortured. They are like LIU Xiaobo, the second person to have been denied the right to have a representative to collect the Nobel Prize and detained by the Chinese government until death.

Hong Kong people have voiced out their demands for freedoms and genuine democracy by participating in peaceful march and rallies. Two million Hongkongers came out and demanded HK government withdraw the extradition law completely. But the government abandoned HK people. Protesters tried different measures such as blocking the roads, conducting strikes and surrounding government buildings but they definitely have acted with great restraint. They just demand the HK government to listen.

Yet the Hong Kong Government refused to listen to the people and abandoned them. In order to pass the bill, the Hong Kong government suppress protests with much violence. The Hong Kong police force hit protestors with baton and shot pepper balls, tear gas and rubber bullets. More terribly, a female first aider was shot in the eye, and might have lost her eyesight and becomes disfigured. At the subway station, riot police chased to a group of allegedly radical protesters, shooting pepper balls in rapid succession at a distance of about two metres from the protesters. At another subway station, there were people who propelled steel beads and lit "smoke cakes", while police fired tear gas at protesters in an indoor area when other innocent civilians are present.

Even though the Chinese communist party and Hong Kong Government suppress Hong Kong protestors, they never give up fighting for freedom and democracy. The freedom fighters of Hong Kong have shown great courage and resilience in their quest for freedom. They endured tear gas and rubber bullets with their modest makeshift protective gears.


(Protestors demonstrating against riot police)


( A first aider was shot in her eye by Hong Kong police)

They showed compassion and humanitarianism to the world by protecting the injured, residents, reporters, such as giving them protective gears and relieving their pain caused by tear gas.


(A protestor help a reporter wash eyes after the riot police leashed tear gas)

Their courage, compassion, resilience and restraint in the past 70 days have turned tides in international politics and set the stage for dialogues between the United States of America and the People's Republic of China towards a peaceful resolution in face of imminent conflict in trade war and regional security issues. Hong Kong freedom fighters deserve the world's recognition and please nominate them for the Nobel Peace Prize 2020.

A little bit more than 240 years ago in the United States, the Founding Fathers fought for their freedom. After 240 years, protestors in Hong Kong are doing the same, fighting for freedom. They abide by universal core values –PEACE, FREEDOM, SOCIAL PROGRESS, EQUAL RIGHTS and HUMAN DIGNITY, same as the United States.




I hope you could nominate the Hong Kong freedom fighters for the 2020 Nobel Peace Prize. Also, I wish you could also support the passing of Hong Kong Human Rights and Democracy Act in the coming September. We also hope that the United States could sanction individual Hong Kong human rights violators pursuant to the Global Magnitsky Act.

Thank you for reading.

Best wishes,
XXXXXXX
請大家寄比自己區既議員/國會議員，並且最好圖文並茂。
